title: What Lies in the Woods
author: Kate Alice Marshall
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "What Lies in the Woods" book by Kate Alice Marshall?
Options:
A. Ethan questioned when this occurred. I responded that I was unsure, however that was untrue. I was fully aware of precisely when the first instance was. And the final one. I took a seat on the motel mattress, my fingers discovering the scar on my wrist. You need not inform me, he uttered. Genuinely. I ought not have inquired. The final instance was eight years prior, I stated, disregarding him. I had returned to town to be with Liv following her release from the infirmary. She refused to converse with me, Kimiko and Marcus would barely speak to anyone, and I had been suffocating in the quietness.
B. Ethan asked when this happened. I said I didn't know, but I was lying. I knew exactly when the first time was. And the last time. I sat on the motel bed, my fingers finding the scar on my wrist. You don't have to tell me, he said. Really. I shouldn't have asked. The last time was eight years ago, I said, ignoring him. I had come back to town to be with Liv after she got out of the hospital. She wouldn't talk to me, Kimiko and Marcus would barely talk to anyone, and I had been suffocating in the silence.  
C.  When was this? Ethan asked. I dont know, I said, but that was a lie. I knew exactly when the first time was. And the last. I sat down on the motel bed, my fingers finding the scar at my wrist. You dont have to tell me, he said. Really. I shouldnt have asked. The last time was eight years ago, I said, ignoring him. Id come back to town to be with Liv after she got out of the hospital. She wouldnt talk to me, Kimiko and Marcus would barely talk to anyone, and Id been suffocating in the silence.
D. Ethan inquired as to when this occurred. I responded that I was uncertain, however that was false. I was completely cognizant of exactly when the initial instance transpired. And the final one. I took a seat on the motel mattress, my fingers detecting the scar on my wrist. You are under no obligation to inform me, he uttered. Genuinely. I should not have queried. She refused to converse with me, Kimiko and Marcus would scarcely speak to anyone, and I had been suffocating in the quietude.
